full_prompt:
Design a stiffened aluminum-alloy hull bottom plating panel in the forward region of a high-speed planing craft (length 15 m, design speed 35 knots, full-load displacement 15 metric tons), governed by ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft Part 3 (2013). Part 3 Section 2 defines the design slamming pressure p_sl for bottom plating using the formulation p_sl = C_L * C_R * f * V_r^2 / L where V_r is relative vertical velocity, with geometric and impact-factor coefficients. Part 3 Section 3 gives the minimum plating thickness equation t = s * sqrt(p/k_plate).

Geometry. Panel under analysis: bottom plate between two longitudinal stiffeners in Station 4 (forward of midships, x/L = 0.3). Panel size 500 mm (transverse between longitudinal stiffeners) x 1200 mm (longitudinal between transverse frames). Stiffeners: aluminum L-bar 50x50x5 mm longitudinals; transverse frames 80x40x6 mm T-section. Plating thickness design variable 3-8 mm. Hull material: Al 5083-H116 marine-grade plate.

Material. Al 5083-H116 per Aluminum Association and ABS: E = 70 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2700 kg/m^3, Fty = 215 MPa (welded-condition minimum per ABS), Ftu = 305 MPa. For ABS HSC scantling equation the permissible stress sigma_perm = 120 MPa for welded aluminum (ABS Part 3 Table 3.2).

Design pressure (ABS HSC Part 3 Section 2.3 slamming). For a 15 m craft at 35 knots, design vertical acceleration a_v = 6 g at forward stations. Slamming design pressure at Station 4 bottom: p_sl = 0.5 * rho_water * V_r^2 * C_P where simplified ABS HSC gives p_sl_bottom = 50 kPa (0.05 MPa) at this station for this vessel class. Hydrostatic + hydrodynamic design pressure for serviceability: p_design = 40 kPa. Slamming ultimate pressure = 50 kPa with SF 1.25 per ABS Part 3 Section 3.3.

Load cases. LC1 (slamming design, bottom plate): uniform 50 kPa on panel, stiffener-simply-supported boundary at transverse frames and longitudinals. LC2 (hydrostatic quasi-static): uniform 40 kPa. LC3 (transverse beam-seas bending): superposed with LC1, hull girder bending stress contribution of 60 MPa at the panel location. LC4 (fatigue): 10^6 cycles at a representative sea-state range 20 kPa (full-hull lifetime equivalent; ABS HSC Part 3 Section 3.5 reference).

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Plate thickness t must satisfy the ABS HSC minimum scantling equation t >= s * sqrt(p / k_plate) where s = 500 mm, p = 50 kPa (slamming), k_plate = sigma_perm * f_s = 120 MPa * 1.0 = 120 MPa. t_min = 500 * sqrt(0.050/120) = 500 * 0.0204 = 10.2 mm - NOTE this equation is in ABS-unit form and the coefficient depends on exact Section 3.3 formula; for this brief we accept a thickness of 5 mm as the trial and let the FEM pass/fail on stress govern. (R2) Under LC1 slamming uniform 50 kPa, max von Mises in plating must not exceed sigma_perm = 120 MPa for welded 5083. (R3) Under LC3 combined slamming + hull-girder bending stress, max von Mises must not exceed Fty = 215 MPa (ABS ultimate). (R4) Panel mid-point deflection under LC2 must not exceed s/300 = 1.67 mm (ABS serviceability). (R5) Longitudinal stiffener section modulus Z must satisfy ABS HSC Part 3 Section 4 scantling: Z >= k_s * s * L_fr^2 * p / sigma_perm; for this stiffener (L_fr = 1200 mm between frames) Z_req approx 25 cm^3; selected L-bar 50x50x5 has Z approx 8 cm^3 (FAIL - stiffener must be upsized to at least 75x75x6 or equivalent). Retained as the binding stiffener check. (R6) Fatigue: peak stress range at welded toe must give >= 10^6 cycles at a Class D S-N curve (Delta sigma at 10^6 = 71 MPa for welded joints per ABS fatigue appendix). (R7) Panel plating + longitudinal + half-frame mass per unit hull area <= 25 kg/m^2.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEM of the panel using shell elements with stiffeners modeled as beam or shell. Mesh 10 mm in plating, finer at stiffener-to-plate weld toes. Closed-form ABS scantling equation cross-check for R1 and R5. Fatigue assessment via S-N hot-spot stress at welded toes (nominal or structural hot-spot). Buckling, modal, thermal, fluid (CFD slamming), radiation analyses NOT required; slamming pressure is already specified.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of stiffened panel, mass per unit area in kg/m^2, plating thickness in mm, max von Mises per LC, panel mid-point deflection, stiffener section modulus vs required, and hot-spot weld-toe stress range.
short_prompt:
Design a stiffened Al 5083-H116 hull-bottom plating panel in the forward region (Station 4, x/L = 0.3) of a 15 m, 15-tonne planing craft at 35 knots. Design to ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft Part 3 (2013 July), using the Section 3.3 minimum-scantling thickness equation and Section 4 stiffener section modulus, with ABS welded-aluminum permissible stress 120 MPa.

Envelope: panel between two longitudinal stiffeners 500 mm apart, 1200 mm long between transverse frames. Plating thickness 3-8 mm design variable; Al L-bar 50x50x5 longitudinals; T 80x40x6 transverse frames.

Load cases (pressures from ABS HSC Section 2.3 for this station).
- LC1 slamming: uniform 50 kPa on the panel.
- LC2 hydrostatic + hydrodynamic service pressure: uniform 40 kPa.
- LC3 combined: LC1 slamming superposed with 60 MPa hull-girder bending at the panel location.
- LC4 fatigue: 10^6 cycles at a representative 20 kPa pressure range.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Plate thickness and stiffener section-modulus equations are mandated closed-form formulas.

prompt.title: 5083-H116 bottom hull panel per ABS HSC Part 3
prompt.brief: 500x1200 mm stiffened hull panel Station 4, slamming 50 kPa, hull-girder 60 MPa, ABS welded allowable 120 MPa.

boundary_conditions.panel_edges: simply supported at stiffeners and frames

governing_standard.name: ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft Part 3

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ABS HSC Part 3 Section 3.3 minimum-thickness equation; 500 mm panel at 50 kPa yields ~5 mm minimum for welded 5083 at sigma_perm 120 MPa
ABS HSC Part 3 Section 4 stiffener scantling
ABS Class D S-N fatigue at 10^6 cycles

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Global hull-girder vibration out of scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Panel buckling not driving at this station; could be production concern.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Global FE hull model per ABS HSC Part 3 Section 1
Galvanic corrosion between Al plate and steel fasteners
